title: Is this Sadamotos artwork
wikitext:
[]: Is this Sadamoto's artwork?             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Sun Jul 27, 2014 9:49 am Sadamoto's artwork or somebody else's? In the past I had it in a folder of Sadamoto pics but looking at it now something makes me wonder if it is him. Someone here will know who it is, yeah?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Jul 27, 2014 10:02 am That's by Kaida Yuji. He's a fairly prolific illustrator that worked on everything from kit and game boxart to poster, article illustrations and card games for franchises like Eva, Macross, Gundam, Godzilla and everything in between.

[]:             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Sun Jul 27, 2014 10:11 am Neat, thank you. ^^ I can't find this picture anywhere in relation to his name on a quick Google img search but the type of colors and the 'thickness' of them in his other ones does fit this pic. Wanted to credit the artist for borrowing the pose/angle in an old (non Eva related) scribble.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Jul 27, 2014 10:31 am The Die Sterne artbook has an index at the back that tells you who the artist is and what the image was made for (magazine, kit, home media, etc) Other Kaida Yuji eva stuff: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Sun Jul 27, 2014 10:36 am Ah I see it now. Awesome. Looks more like acrylics he's using. Cheers for that.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 27, 2014 12:37 pm Reichu did a translation/index of the art from Die Sterne, might be useful for stuff like this: http://www.evacommentary.org/omake/die-sterne/die-sterne.html

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Sun Jul 27, 2014 7:28 pm so im unsure if that MP eva image is a strike witches parody or strike witches parodied this

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sun Jul 27, 2014 8:29 pm Rosenakahara wrote: so im unsure if that MP eva image is a strike witches parody or strike witches parodied this

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Jul 28, 2014 4:19 am A.T. Fish wrote: If you mean this it's a parody of a famous WWII picture.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Jul 28, 2014 8:20 pm Of a Japanese defeat no less.
